name: AVAudioEngine Multi-Track Mixing (DJ / Audio Production)
antiPatterns: Using AVAudioPlayer for multi-track mixing — AVAudioPlayer cannot mix, crossfade, or apply real-time effects; use AVAudioEngine with AVAudioPlayerNode
Calling engine.connect() before engine.attach() — attach all nodes first, then connect
Accessing floatChannelData without optional chaining — it returns UnsafePointer<UnsafeMutablePointer<Float>>? and force-unwrapping crashes at runtime
Using the deprecated UIDocumentPickerViewController(documentTypes:in:) initializer — use UIDocumentPickerViewController(forOpeningContentTypes:) with UTType
Forgetting url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() after picking a document — without it the audio file cannot be read from the sandbox
Forgetting to set eq.bands[n].bypass = false — AVAudioUnitEQ bands are bypassed by default and have no effect until explicitly enabled
Wrong EQ connection order — must be playerNode → eq → mixer → mainMixer, not playerNode → mixer → eq
Passing raw Color to Canvas context.fill/stroke with: parameter — use .color(Color.green) not Color.green directly
Using playerNode.nodeTime(forHostTime:) to get current time — this method does not exist; use AVAudioTime(hostTime: mach_absolute_time()) to create a current-time AVAudioTime value

Passing a UInt64 to the `at:` parameter of schedule(buffer:at:options:) or schedule(file:at:options:) — the `at:` parameter takes AVAudioTime?, not a raw host time integer; pass nil to play immediately or AVAudioTime(hostTime: mach_absolute_time()) to play now

Using AVAudioPlayerNode.rate for tempo changes — it only works when connected with a non-nil format matching the file's processing format; use AVAudioUnitTimePitch instead, which works reliably in all configurations

Forgetting to configure AVAudioSession.sharedInstance().setCategory(.playback) before starting AVAudioEngine — without this, iOS silently blocks audio output and playback appears broken even though the engine and player node are running correctly

Using .onTapGesture or custom gesture modifiers on shapes for important interactive buttons — parent gestures, ZStack layers, and overlapping gesture areas can silently swallow taps; always use plain SwiftUI Button(action:) { } views for buttons that must reliably respond to touch
Wrapping multiple buttons in a shared DragGesture container to detect sliding between them — this breaks all tap events on the child buttons; use independent buttons with shared state logic instead

Putting AVAudioFramePosition, AVAudioFrameCount, or other AVFoundation types in View files without importing AVFoundation — these types are not automatically available from SwiftUI alone; add import AVFoundation to any file that references them
Using fixed pixel heights for layouts that need to work across different iPhone sizes in landscape — use GeometryReader with proportional sizing and .minimumScaleFactor for text that might clip
Displaying BPM or other numeric values without .lineLimit(1) and .fixedSize(horizontal: true, vertical: false) — numeric displays should never wrap to multiple lines

Do NOT use LongPressGesture for hold-to-play or hold-to-preview features. LongPressGesture fires a single completion event after the minimum duration and then ends — it does not continuously track whether the finger is still held down. Use DragGesture(minimumDistance: 0) instead. Its .onChanged fires immediately on finger down and .onEnded fires on finger up, giving true hold/release behavior.
